description: Port the treb run command's human-readable output from Rust's current format to exact Go CLI visual parity, replacing tree-grouped deployments and numbered transaction lists with Go's section-based format (Transactions, Collisions, Deployment Summary, Script Logs, Registry Update, Success message).

userStories.title: Restructure display_result_human Section Ordering and Add Section Headers
userStories.description: As a developer, I want display_result_human() restructured to follow Go's RenderExecution output order with consistent section headers so that subsequent stories can fill in each section's content.
acceptanceCriteria: display_result_human() calls sections in order: transactions, collisions, deployment summary, logs, registry update
A print_section_header(emoji, title, separator_width) helper renders bold header + gray separator consistently
Summary line block is removed
DRY RUN banner is removed from display_result_human (dry-run state conveyed via pre-execution banner Mode field)
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets passes
Typecheck passes (cargo check -p treb-cli)

userStories.title: Port Transaction Section with Status Colors and Operation Info
userStories.description: As a user, I want each transaction displayed with a colored status string, green sender name, and operation description followed by a gray footer with hash/block/gas so that the output matches Go's transaction rendering.
acceptanceCriteria: Transaction status is colored per Go palette (simulated=faint, queued=yellow, executed=green, failed=red, unknown=white) with fixed-width padding
Sender name is displayed in green
Operations from RecordedTransaction.transaction.operations are used to show call info
Footer shows hash, block number, and gas in gray, pipe-separated, only when non-empty
Empty transaction list shows gray fallback message
Typecheck passes (cargo check -p treb-cli)

userStories.title: Port Deployment Summary and Collision Sections
userStories.description: As a user, I want deployments shown as a flat list with cyan names and green addresses, and collisions shown with yellow warning headers and per-collision details, so that the output matches Go's format.
acceptanceCriteria: Deployment summary shows flat list with cyan contract names and green addresses
Label appended as :{label} format when present
Proxy deployments show [{impl}] suffix
Collisions show yellow warning header with per-collision output including contract name and address
Collision label and entropy shown only when non-empty
Empty deployments section is omitted entirely
Typecheck passes (cargo check -p treb-cli)

userStories.title: Port Console Logs, Registry Update, and Success Message
userStories.description: As a user, I want console logs displayed with a bold emoji header and 2-space indented lines, registry update confirmation with green/yellow coloring, and the final success message matching Go's format.
acceptanceCriteria: Console logs section has bold emoji header + 40-char gray separator + 2-space indented lines
Console logs section only appears when logs exist
Registry update message shows in green when successful with changes
Registry update message shows in yellow when no changes
Registry update only appears when not dry-run and execution succeeded
Final success message reads '✓ Script execution completed successfully' in green
Typecheck passes (cargo check -p treb-cli)

userStories.title: Port Pre-Execution Deployment Banner (Verbose Mode)
userStories.description: As a user running verbose mode, I want the pre-execution banner to show Go's PrintDeploymentBanner format with emoji header, dash separators, and per-field coloring so that the output matches Go's layout.
acceptanceCriteria: Pre-execution banner shows '🚀 Running Deployment Script' in bold with 50-char dash separators
Script name is cyan, network name is blue, chain ID in gray parentheses
Namespace is magenta
Mode is colored by type (DRY_RUN=yellow, FORK=magenta, LIVE=green)
Env vars show key=value with yellow keys and green values, aligned
Senders shown in gray
Typecheck passes (cargo check -p treb-cli)

userStories.title: Port Governor Proposals Display and Debug Output Format
userStories.description: As a user, I want the governor proposals section formatted with a bold emoji header and colored per-proposal details consistent with Go's transaction rendering style.
acceptanceCriteria: Governor proposals section has bold emoji header with separator consistent with other sections
Per-proposal shows proposal ID, governor/timelock addresses, status, and linked transaction count
Skipped deployments section matches Go format or is removed if Go handles via collisions only
Debug log file writes successfully with updated format
Typecheck passes (cargo check -p treb-cli)

userStories.title: Update All Golden Test Expected Files
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all 14 run-related golden test expected files regenerated to match the new Go-parity output format so that the full test suite passes.
acceptanceCriteria: All 14 run golden test directories produce passing tests
Golden file diffs show expected format changes (section headers, separators, removed summary line)
cargo test -p treb-cli --test integration_run passes without UPDATE_GOLDEN
cargo test -p treb-cli --test cli_run passes (argument parsing unaffected)
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets passes
No regressions in other test suites: cargo test -p treb-cli passes fully
